narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout
narratives.thesis: Demand for high-performance AI chips and related infrastructure continues to drive strong revenue growth for semiconductor manufacturers and equipment providers.
watchFor: NVDA data center revenue growth decelerates below 50% YoY
Major hyperscalers (MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL) signal capex cuts for AI
New US export controls significantly impact TSM or ASML sales to China

narratives.name: AI Software & Cloud Adoption
narratives.thesis: Enterprises are increasingly adopting AI-powered software and cloud services, driving growth for companies providing data platforms, cybersecurity, and AI integration tools.
watchFor: Major cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) report significant deceleration in cloud growth
Enterprise software spending surveys show a decline in AI-related budgets
High-profile AI project failures lead to enterprise disillusionment

narratives.name: GLP-1 Obesity Wave
narratives.thesis: The market for GLP-1 obesity and diabetes drugs continues to expand rapidly, driving significant revenue growth and investor interest in leading manufacturers.
watchFor: LLY reports unexpected safety concerns or trial failures for GLP-1 drugs
Insurance coverage for GLP-1s is significantly curtailed
New, more effective non-GLP-1 obesity treatments emerge

narratives.name: Digital Ad & Content Dominance
narratives.thesis: Leading internet platforms continue to dominate digital advertising and content consumption, leveraging AI for better targeting and engagement.
watchFor: GOOGL reports significant decline in search ad revenue growth
Netflix reports subscriber losses in key markets
RDDT ARPU growth decelerates sharply

narratives.name: Discount Stores Resilience
narratives.thesis: In a high-inflation environment, consumers continue to seek value, driving strong performance for discount retailers.
watchFor: WMT or COST report unexpected decline in same-store sales
Core CPI drops below 2.5% YoY, reducing pressure on consumer budgets
Competitors aggressively cut prices, eroding margins

narratives.name: Enterprise AI Hardware Demand
narratives.thesis: Beyond core chips, the demand for specialized hardware to run AI models in enterprise data centers is growing, benefiting server and networking equipment providers.
watchFor: DELL or ANET report significant order cancellations or delays from enterprise customers
Hyperscalers begin to insource more of their AI hardware development
Global supply chain disruptions impact hardware component availability

narratives.name: Cybersecurity Expansion
narratives.thesis: Increased digital transformation and AI adoption necessitate robust cybersecurity solutions, driving demand for advanced threat protection and zero-trust architectures.
watchFor: Major cybersecurity breaches at PANW/ZS customers erode confidence
Increased competition from large tech players (MSFT, GOOGL) in enterprise security
Enterprise IT spending surveys show a shift away from security investments

narratives.name: E-commerce Resilience
narratives.thesis: Consumers continue to favor online shopping, especially for value, benefiting established e-commerce platforms and discount retailers.
watchFor: Retail sales data shows significant deceleration in online spending
COST or WMT report same-store sales decline
Consumer discretionary spending shifts heavily to experiences over goods

narratives.name: Streaming Content Expansion
narratives.thesis: Streaming services continue to expand their reach and offerings, with Netflix leading the charge in ad-supported tiers and global subscriber growth.
watchFor: NFLX ad-supported tier growth stalls or reverses
Increased competition from rival streamers leads to price wars
Content production costs escalate beyond subscriber revenue growth

narratives.name: Logistics Efficiency & AI
narratives.thesis: Logistics companies are investing in AI and efficiency improvements to optimize operations and meet demand, benefiting those with strong execution.
watchFor: FDX reports significant decline in package volumes or margins
Fuel prices spike, eroding logistics profitability
Global economic slowdown impacts freight demand

narratives.name: Credit Services Strength
narratives.thesis: Strong consumer spending and robust credit card activity continue to benefit payment processors and credit card companies.
watchFor: Consumer spending data shows significant contraction
AXP reports unexpected decline in cardmember spending or loan growth
Unemployment rate rises sharply, impacting credit quality

narratives.name: Residential Construction Headwinds
narratives.thesis: Rising mortgage rates and potential lawsuits are creating significant headwinds for the residential construction sector, impacting new home sales and builder sentiment.
watchFor: 30-year fixed mortgage rates drop below 6%
Housing starts data shows unexpected rebound
Government stimulus programs for first-time homebuyers announced

narratives.name: Investment Banking & AI Deals
narratives.thesis: Major investment banks are benefiting from a resurgence in M&A and IPO activity, particularly in the AI and tech sectors, driving advisory fees.
watchFor: Global M&A activity significantly declines
Major tech IPOs are delayed or underperform
Regulatory crackdown on SPACs or private market valuations

narratives.name: Home Improvement Demand
narratives.thesis: Despite housing market headwinds, consumers continue to invest in home improvement, benefiting retailers offering DIY products and services.
watchFor: LOW reports significant decline in pro sales segment
Consumer spending surveys show sharp drop in home renovation budgets
Existing home sales plummet, reducing new homeowner renovation activity

narratives.name: Gold
narratives.thesis: Gold acts as a hedge against inflation and geopolitical uncertainty, but its price is sensitive to real yields and dollar strength.
watchFor: 10Y real yields turn positive and rise above 1%
US Dollar Index (DXY) breaks above 108
Major central banks signal aggressive rate hikes

narratives.name: Aerospace & Defense Spending
narratives.thesis: Geopolitical tensions continue to support strong defense spending, benefiting aerospace and defense contractors.
watchFor: Major defense budget cuts announced by US or NATO allies
De-escalation of major geopolitical conflicts
GD reports significant program delays or cost overruns

narratives.name: Silver
narratives.thesis: Silver's price is influenced by both its safe-haven appeal (like gold) and industrial demand, making it sensitive to economic growth and inflation.
watchFor: Global industrial production data shows significant contraction
Gold prices decline sharply, dragging silver lower
New supply of silver comes online unexpectedly

narratives.name: AI Optics Profit-Taking
narratives.thesis: After a significant run, some AI optics stocks are experiencing profit-taking, suggesting a temporary cooling or re-evaluation of valuation in this specific segment.
watchFor: AAOI reports strong earnings and positive guidance, reversing sentiment
New major AI optics contracts announced for AAOI
Broader AI infrastructure buildout accelerates unexpectedly

Technology.thesis: The Technology sector remains broadly bullish, primarily driven by the insatiable demand for AI infrastructure and related software. While some profit-taking is observed in specific AI-adjacent areas, the core thesis of expanding AI capex and adoption continues to fuel growth across semiconductors, software, and hardware.
watchFor: Hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Core CPI print > 0.4% MoM for two consecutive months — forces hawkish Fed repricing, hurting growth multiples
China export controls expand to advanced AI chips (e.g., HBM, advanced packaging)

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: The Consumer Cyclical sector is mixed. While e-commerce and discount retail show resilience due to value-seeking consumers, residential construction faces headwinds from rising rates, and luxury/discretionary spending is under pressure from inflation and changing consumer habits.
watchFor: Fed signals rate cuts — would boost housing and discretionary spending
Core PCE inflation drops below 2.5% YoY — eases consumer spending pressure
Consumer confidence index drops below 90 — signals broader spending slowdown

Communication Services.thesis: The Communication Services sector is mixed. While digital advertising and streaming continue to evolve with AI integration, traditional content companies face geopolitical headwinds and competition, leading to a bifurcated outlook.
watchFor: Major ad spending reports show significant deceleration in digital channels
Netflix subscriber growth stalls or declines for two consecutive quarters
Regulatory action against large tech platforms impacts ad revenue models

Industrials.thesis: The Industrials sector is mixed. While logistics companies are benefiting from efficiency drives and defense spending remains robust, heavy machinery and electrical equipment face varying demand signals, with some AI-related tailwinds but also broader economic uncertainty.
watchFor: Global trade volume data shows significant contraction
Defense spending cuts announced in major economies
ISM Manufacturing PMI drops below 45 for two consecutive months

Healthcare.thesis: The Healthcare sector is mixed. While the GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive significant interest and AI integration promises R&D acceleration, broader healthcare plans and generic pharma face regulatory and competitive pressures.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors enter market with superior efficacy/safety profile
Regulatory pushback on GLP-1 drug pricing or insurance coverage
Major healthcare policy changes impacting drug manufacturers or health plans

Financials.thesis: The Financials sector is mixed. While credit services and investment banks show strength driven by consumer spending and AI-related deal flow, diversified banks face ongoing regulatory scrutiny and interest rate uncertainty.
watchFor: Fed signals aggressive rate cuts — compressing bank net interest margins
Significant increase in credit card delinquencies or loan defaults
New financial regulations impacting bank profitability or capital requirements

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The Consumer Defensive sector, particularly discount stores, remains bullish as consumers prioritize value in an inflationary environment, driving consistent demand and strong sales for essential goods.
watchFor: Inflation cools significantly, shifting consumer spending to discretionary items
WMT or COST report unexpected decline in foot traffic or sales
Consumer confidence index surges, indicating willingness to spend more on non-essentials

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are mixed. While inflation concerns and geopolitical tensions provide some support, the prospect of an Iran deal and fluctuating Treasury yields create uncertainty, leading to volatile price action.
watchFor: US Dollar Index (DXY) strengthens significantly, hurting dollar-denominated assets
Real yields (10Y Treasury minus CPI) rise sharply
Major de-escalation of geopolitical conflicts globally

The ‘stunning’ end to Trump’s case against the IRS raises tax-law questions that will be analyzed for years

Bezos defends billionaires, hypes AI, talks taxes and praises Trump in CNBC interview
Congress is best chance to stop Trump 'lawfare' fund, attorneys say

SEC Proposes Transformative Reforms to Help Public Companies Conduct Registered Offerings and Simplify Reporting Requirements

CPI for all items rises 0.6% in April; shelter and gasoline up
Payroll employment edges up by 115,000 in April; unemployment rate unchanged at 4.3%
SEC Charges 21 Individuals With Alleged Wide-Reaching Insider Trading Scheme

Federal Reserve Board requests public comment on a proposal to establish a "payment account," which legally eligible financial institutions could use for the specific purpose of clearing and settling their payments
Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee, April 28-29, 2026
Federal Reserve Board names Jerome H. Powell as chair pro tempore; Powell will serve as chair pro tempore until Kevin M. Warsh is sworn in as the new chair
Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by the Stephen M. Calk 2025 Trust

Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement actions with UBS Group AG, Credit Suisse AG, Credit Suisse Holdings (USA), Inc., and Credit Suisse AG, New York Branch
